sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.16 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Following Yu.\,I.\,Merzlyakov we define the width\/ of a verbal subgroup$V(G)$of a group$G$with respect to the set of words$V$as the smallest$m { N} \{ \}$such that every element of$V(G)$can be written as a product of$m$values of words from$V V^{-1}$. It is known that the width of any verbal subgroup of a finitely generated group of polynomial growth is finite. Is this statement true for finitely ...
